source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Tina draws shapes into each field of the pyramid. Each field in the second and third row contains exactly the shapes of the two fields below. Some fields are already done. Which shapes does she draw into the empty field of the bottom row? <image1>
<image2>
extraction_notes: options field contains only choice labels without option text, so raw_answer_text is kept as the original answer label

issues: question_text references 2 image placeholders ('<image1>' and '<image2>') but only 1 image path is provided

image_dependency: Question asks about shapes in fields of a pyramid and which option matches the empty bottom field, which requires seeing the diagram and answer choices.
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the sample is primarily visual rather than solvable from text alone.
The needed information includes spatial arrangement and visual contents of fields, not fully specified in the text.
multi_step: The text gives a compositional rule: each field in the second and third row contains exactly the shapes of the two fields below.
Solving requires combining information across multiple pyramid cells to infer the missing bottom field, then matching that inferred set of shapes to one of the options.
This is a joint text-plus-image reasoning task rather than a single direct lookup.
verifiability: The answer is a single multiple-choice option (answer_type=option, answer_text='D'), which is easy to compare against a derived result.
The rule in the prompt is explicit and deterministic, so a checker could verify whether the inferred bottom field is consistent with the shown higher rows.
asset_registry_record shows answer_present=true and registry_passed=true, supporting basic evaluability, though there is an image placeholder mismatch issue.
